title: Counter state is fully permissionless and can be arbitrarily rewritten by any caller
reason: `Counter.sol` is a trivial sample contract whose only state is intentionally public and mutable; no realistic protocol-level harm is evidenced from this codebase.

title: Hardcoded external addresses are used without chain or code validation
reason: This is a deployment/configuration assumption in a purpose-built verifier, not an exploitable in-protocol vulnerability; misdeploying to the wrong chain is operator error rather than a reportable bug.

title: The exploit path never verifies that the token-side corruption actually happened before swapping
reason: If the assumed token bug does not occur, the downstream Uniswap swap reverts and the transaction rolls back; the code does not create a distinct, realistic loss or lockup beyond already-reported liveness issues.
